text:
For
 
:
 
Hithashree
 
P
  
|
 
hithupurushotham26@gmail.com
 
50
 
Days
 
Web
 
Dev
 
Challenge:
 
Official
 
Participant
 
Guide
 
Welcome
 
to
 
the
 
50
 
Days
 
Web
 
Development
 
Challenge!
 
We
 
are
 
thrilled
 
to
 
have
 
you
 
on
 
board.
 
This
 
document
 
serves
 
as
 
your
 
daily
 
quick-reference
 
guide
 
for
 
participating
 
in
 
the
 
challenge,
 
submitting
 
your
 
tasks,
 
and
 
tracking
 
your
 
progress.
 
1.
 
Daily
 
Workflow
 
●
 
Task
 
Release:
 
A
 
new
 
web
 
development
 
task
 
will
 
be
 
published
 
every
 
day
 
at
 
exactly
 
8:00
 
PM
.
 
●
 
Where
 
to
 
Find
 
It:
 
All
 
tasks
 
are
 
hosted
 
on
 
our
 
central
 
GitHub
 
repository:
 
Synexus-Core/50-Days-Dev-Challenge
 
●
 
Folder
 
Structure:
 
Navigate
 
to
 
the
 
specific
 
folder
 
for
 
the
 
day
 
(e.g.,
 
Day-01
,
 
Day-02
).
 
Inside,
 
you
 
will
 
find
 
a
 
readme.md
 
file
 
containing
 
the
 
detailed
 
prompt,
 
constraints,
 
and
 
learning
 
objectives,
 
alongside
 
a
 
helper
 
file
 
containing
 
the
 
initial
 
boilerplate
 
template.
 
2.
 
Submission
 
Guidelines
 
Consistency
 
is
 
key.
 
To
 
successfully
 
complete
 
a
 
daily
 
task
 
and
 
get
 
it
 
evaluated:
 
●
 
Deadline:
 
You
 
must
 
complete
 
the
 
task
 
and
 
push
 
your
 
code
 
to
 
your
 
own
 
GitHub
 
repository
 
by
 
the
 
End
 
of
 
Day
 
(EOD)
 
the
 
following
 
day
.
 
●
 
Proof
 
of
 
Work:
 
Share
 
your
 
daily
 
progress
 
on
 
LinkedIn
 
to
 
build
 
your
 
public
 
portfolio.
 
○
 
Briefly
 
explain
 
what
 
you
 
learned
 
or
 
built.
 
○
 
Use
 
the
 
sample
 
caption
 
provided
 
at
 
the
 
bottom
 
of
 
the
 
daily
 
readme.md
 
file.
 
○
 
Mandatory:
 
Tag
 
Synexus
 
in
 
your
 
post
 
and
 
use
 
the
 
official
 
hashtags
 
provided
 
in
 
the
 
repository
 
to
 
ensure
 
we
 
can
 
track
 
your
 
submission.
 
3.
 
Getting
 
Started
 
&
 
Prerequisites
 
If
 
you
 
are
 
new
 
to
 
Git,
 
GitHub,
 
or
 
the
 
required
 
tech
 
stack,
 
do
 
not
 
worry.
 
Before
 
starting
 
your
 
first
 
task,
 
please
 
visit
 
the
 
Getting
 
Started
 
folder
.
 
It
 
contains:
 
●
 
Instructions
 
on
 
installing
 
necessary
 
software.
 
●
 
Recommended
 
IDEs
 
(Integrated
 
Development
 
Environments)
 
and
 
extensions.
 
●
 
A
 
brief
 
guide
 
on
 
how
 
to
 
clone
 
repositories
 
and
 
push
 
your
 
code.
 
4.
 
Code
 
of
 
Conduct
 
●
 
Do
 
not
 
copy
 
code
 
directly
 
from
 
peers.
 
Use
 
the
 
helper
 
files,
 
consult
 
documentation,
 
and
 
build
 
it
 
yourself.
 
●
 
If
 
you
 
get
 
stuck,
 
collaborate!
 
The
 
goal
 
of
 
this
 
challenge
 
is
 
not
 
just
 
to
 
code,
 
but
 
to
 
learn
 
how
 
to
 
solve
 
problems
 
as
 
a
 
community.
 
Best
 
of
 
luck.
 
We
 
look
 
forward
 
to
 
seeing
 
the
 
incredible
 
projects
 
you
 
will
 
build
 
over
 
the
 
next
 
50
 
days.
 
Happy
 
Coding!
 
 
For
 
:
 
Hithashree
 
P
  
|
 
hithupurushotham26@gmail.com
 
Team
 
Synexus
 
 
Standard,
 
not
 
a
 
trend.
 
Logic,
 
not
 
a
 
language.
